title: portulaca ferricola
intro: Portulaca ferricola, also known as the New Zealand portulaca or pink purslane, is a succulent plant species native to New Zealand. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as its ability to support the immune system and exhibit antifungal properties. Portulaca ferricola is prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
